description: sc-13134 cross-language golden fixtures for the Style composer (Subject:/Style: directive-collision splice). Consumed by BOTH apps/web/src/styleComposer.test.js (composeStyledPrompt) AND crates/sceneworks-core/src/style_composer.rs (compose_styled_prompt) so the JS composer and its Rust port produce byte-identical `expected` output. A null `styleText` means no style was selected (identity pass-through). Add a case here to pin new behavior in both languages at once.

cases.name: no style selected -> passthrough unchanged
cases.expected: a fox in the snow

cases.name: null styleText behaves as no style -> passthrough
cases.expected: a fox in the snow

cases.name: whitespace-only styleText behaves as no style -> passthrough
cases.expected: a fox in the snow

cases.name: clean prompt -> Subject leads, Style trails
cases.expected:
Subject: a fox in the snow
Style: cinematic watercolor

cases.name: mid-sentence colon is NOT a directive
cases.userPrompt: a portrait: dramatic lighting on her face
cases.expected:
Subject: a portrait: dramatic lighting on her face
Style: oil painting

cases.name: lowercase line-start colon is NOT a directive
cases.expected:
Subject: style: not really a directive
Style: oil painting

cases.name: unrecognized capitalized key is NOT a directive (folds into Subject)
cases.userPrompt: Note: this is a plain sentence
cases.expected:
Subject: Note: this is a plain sentence
Style: oil painting

cases.name: recognized key mid-line is NOT a directive (stays prose)
cases.userPrompt: a photo with dramatic Lighting: soft
cases.expected:
Subject: a photo with dramatic Lighting: soft
Style: oil painting

cases.name: recognized key after leading word is NOT a directive (stays prose)
cases.expected:
Subject: The Setting: was perfect
Style: oil painting

cases.name: CRLF directive lines classify correctly, no stray carriage return
cases.expected:
Subject: something
Style: noir
Setting: alley
Angle: low

cases.name: CRLF prose-then-directive splices cleanly
cases.userPrompt:
a detective
Setting: a foggy alley
cases.expected:
Subject: a detective
Style: noir
Setting: a foggy alley

cases.name: foreign directive preserved as sibling, remainder -> Subject
cases.userPrompt:
a detective in the rain
Setting: a foggy alley
cases.expected:
Subject: a detective in the rain
Style: noir
Setting: a foggy alley

cases.name: multiple foreign directives preserved as siblings in order
cases.userPrompt:
a detective
Setting: a foggy alley
Angle: low angle
Lighting: single streetlamp
cases.expected:
Subject: a detective
Style: noir
Setting: a foggy alley
Angle: low angle
Lighting: single streetlamp

cases.name: own Style line merges - catalog first, user words appended after comma
cases.userPrompt:
Style: loose brushwork
a castle on a hill
cases.expected:
Subject: a castle on a hill
Style: oil painting, loose brushwork

cases.name: own Style line merges alongside other foreign directives
cases.userPrompt:
Style: high contrast
Setting: alley
a detective
cases.expected:
Subject: a detective
Style: noir, high contrast
Setting: alley

cases.name: own Subject line folds into the prose without doubling the label
cases.userPrompt:
Subject: a detective
under a streetlamp
cases.expected:
Subject: a detective
under a streetlamp
Style: noir

cases.name: own Subject line keeps line order alongside surrounding prose
cases.userPrompt:
in the rain
Subject: a detective
Setting: alley
cases.expected:
Subject: in the rain
a detective
Style: noir
Setting: alley

cases.name: own bare Subject line (no user words) is dropped, no blank line
cases.expected:
Subject: a detective
Style: noir

cases.name: own Description line stays a sibling (not folded into Subject)
cases.userPrompt:
a detective
Description: shot on film
cases.expected:
Subject: a detective
Style: noir
Description: shot on film

cases.name: empty userPrompt with a style -> Style only, no Subject
cases.expected: Style: cinematic

cases.name: whitespace-only userPrompt with a style -> Style only, no Subject
cases.expected: Style: cinematic

cases.name: styleText is trimmed before templating
cases.expected:
Subject: a fox
Style: cinematic watercolor

cases.name: unicode content is preserved in Subject
cases.expected:
Subject: 富士山と桜、朝の光
Style: 浮世絵

cases.name: unicode prose alongside a foreign directive
cases.userPrompt:
富士山と桜
Setting: 早朝
cases.expected:
Subject: 富士山と桜
Style: 浮世絵
Setting: 早朝

cases.name: leading whitespace before a directive key still detects it
cases.expected:
Subject: a detective
Style: noir
Angle: low

cases.name: directive-only prompt (no prose) -> Style + siblings, no Subject
cases.expected:
Style: noir
Setting: alley
Angle: low

cases.name: own bare Style line (no user words) does not append a trailing comma
cases.expected:
Subject: a castle
Style: oil painting

cases.name: multi-line prose remainder is preserved across lines
cases.userPrompt:
a detective
under a streetlamp
Setting: alley
cases.expected:
Subject: a detective
under a streetlamp
Style: noir
Setting: alley
